name: Loser's Eating House
notes: Pre-order only via weekly Instagram drops; sells out instantly.
review: "Face-sized sourdough brioche with olive oil frosting, flaky salt, and glaze oozing from every layer — at $21 it feeds six."

name: American Bar
notes: Weekend brunch only; croissant-style bun ($10).
review: "Croissant-like warm rolls with cream cheese frosting on the side so you can lather on as much as you desire."

name: Little Red Kitchen Bake Shop
notes: Weekend-only; sells out fast — preorder online.
review: "Milk bread dough with a silky, subtle cream cheese frosting and lots of swirls — optimal ratio of dough to filling."

name: Ole & Steen - Upper East Side
notes: Danish chain; shareable pull-apart braid, not a traditional roll.
review: "Braided in classic Danish style — lighter and less sweet than American counterparts, perfect with a black coffee."

name: Ole & Steen - Midtown East
notes: Danish chain; shareable pull-apart braid, not a traditional roll.
review: "Braided in classic Danish style — lighter and less sweet than American counterparts, perfect with a black coffee."

name: Ole & Steen - Bryant Park
notes: Danish chain; shareable pull-apart braid, not a traditional roll.
review: "Braided in classic Danish style — lighter and less sweet than American counterparts, perfect with a black coffee."

name: Ole & Steen - Union Square
notes: Danish chain; shareable pull-apart braid, not a traditional roll.
review: "Braided in classic Danish style — lighter and less sweet than American counterparts, perfect with a black coffee."

name: Barachou - West Village
notes: Newer location (2022); French cream puff shop.
review: "Sleeper-hit cinnamon roll at a cream puff shop — doughy and gooey with a thin vanilla glaze that complements rather than overwhelms."

name: Barachou - Upper West Side
notes: The original (2019); French cream puff shop.
review: "Sleeper-hit cinnamon roll at a cream puff shop — doughy and gooey with a thin vanilla glaze that complements rather than overwhelms."

name: Hani's Bakery & Café
notes: Gramercy Tavern alum; malted bun is the bestseller.
review: "So expertly frosted you can't see the dough underneath — malted milk cream cheese frosting with just an idea of cardamom."

name: Serano Bakery
notes: Greek-American bakery; open late until 10:30pm+.
review: "A 4.9-star Astoria favorite — traditional, homey pastries and a rare evening cinnamon roll option in Queens."

name: Bakeri - Williamsburg
notes: Scratch-made; sells out fast — arrive early.
review: "Dense and gooey with a delicious, thick layer of frosting — a charming neighborhood café where everything is made from scratch."

name: Bakeri - Greenpoint
notes: Scratch-made; sells out fast — arrive early.
review: "Dense and gooey with a delicious, thick layer of frosting — a charming neighborhood café where everything is made from scratch."

name: Leland Eating & Drinking House
notes: Full-service restaurant; standout orange zest cinnamon roll.
review: "You've gotta try the orange zest cinnamon rolls — come for the roll, stay for the full menu."

name: Mah-Ze-Dahr - Downtown
notes: They call it a sticky bun; weekends only.
review: "Deliciously sweet and cinnamony with smooth frosting drizzled on top — as close to the platonic ideal of a cinnamon roll as you can get."

name: Winner - Park Slope
notes: Flagship; kouign-amann-style roll, daily 7–11am.
review: "Sticky like a kouign-amann with cream cheese frosting spiked with lemon juice — the caramelized underside tastes like caramel."

name: Winner - Prospect Park
notes: Seasonal park kiosk; check hours before visiting.
review: "Sticky like a kouign-amann with cream cheese frosting spiked with lemon juice — the caramelized underside tastes like caramel."

name: Michaeli Bakery
notes: Israeli-American bakery; closed Saturdays.
review: "A hidden gem alongside their famous babka — the pastries reflect Israeli and American baking traditions beautifully."

name: Super Nice - East Harlem
notes: Rare quality cinnamon roll option above 96th Street.
review: "Great coffee and fresh-baked pastries in a warm, no-frills setting — perfect for a low-key morning treat."

name: Super Nice - Manhattan Valley
notes: Rare quality cinnamon roll option above 96th Street.
review: "Great coffee and fresh-baked pastries in a warm, no-frills setting — perfect for a low-key morning treat."

name: Rigor Hill Market
notes: Rated 9.7/10 by The Carboholic; frozen pans available ($25/8).
review: "Light and airy, almost like a croissant, with a thin icing on top that shatters like peanut brittle."

name: The Noortwyck
notes: Weekend brunch only; Eleven Madison Park alum team.
review: "Generously frosted, warm, and buttery — a fine-dining pedigree pastry that rivals dedicated bakeries at brunch-friendly prices."

name: Petit Chou
notes: Eclair specialist; check Instagram for seasonal flavors.
review: "Light and fluffy with a conservative but well-balanced cinnamon filling — a solid option, especially paired with one of their eclairs."

name: Peter Pan Donut & Pastry Shop
notes: NYC institution since 1953; doors open at 4:30am daily.
review: "Fried donut dough dusted with cinnamon, slathered with glaze, and topped with homemade buttercream — the value is impossible to beat."

name: Spirals
notes: Dedicated cinnamon roll shop; each topped with a spiral sugar cookie.
review: "Expertly fluffy with equal parts cinnamon glaze and cream cheese frosting — say yes when they ask if you want yours warmed."

name: KYU
notes: Wood-fired restaurant; $15 dessert, not a bakery stop.
review: "Soupy black sugar innards frosted with a chai-flavored glaze, served warm — transformational."

name: Sunday Morning - East Village
notes: The original; 10 rotating flavors daily, sells out early.
review: "The Chocolate Babka roll is light, fluffy, and memorable — arrive before 11am for the best selection."

name: Sunday Morning - NoMad
notes: Newer location; same flavors, less hectic than the original.
review: "The Chocolate Babka roll is light, fluffy, and memorable — arrive before 11am for the best selection."

name: Red Gate Bakery
notes: Brown butter rolls Wed/Thu; specialty flavors Fri/Sat.
review: "Crisp golden-brown edges with a signature drizzle of brown butter-cinnamon cinnyGOO on top — limited quantity, check Instagram."

name: Bourke Street Bakery
notes: Australian-founded; pastries ship nationally via Goldbelly.
review: "Less frosting-heavy than American versions, more about the dough and spice — a reliable daily option with a strong pastry program."

name: Ceremonia Bakeshop
notes: Closed Mon–Tue; also famous for their breakfast burrito.
review: "Like a collaboration between a classic cinnamon bun and a Krispy Kreme donut — glazed, pillowy, and addictive."

name: L'Appartement 4F - Brooklyn Heights
notes: The original; laminated roll rated 9.5/10 by The Carboholic.
review: "Crisp and flaky with a butter-forward personality and a thick gob of cream cheese icing — elegant enough for a solo pastry outing."

name: L'Appartement 4F - West Village
notes: Newer expansion (Feb 2025); same laminated roll.
review: "Crisp and flaky with a butter-forward personality and a thick gob of cream cheese icing — elegant enough for a solo pastry outing."

name: Baked
notes: Red Hook staple; dense roll with caramelized sugar bottom.
review: "The sugar gets crunchy and caramelized toward the bottom — a great pastry to enjoy with your morning coffee."

name: Ciao Gloria
notes: From a Baked alum; sells out daily — come early.
review: "If a cinnamon roll emoji existed, it might look like this one — fluffy dough, filling straight from the cinnamon tree, cream cheese icing worthy of a carrot cake."

name: Breads Bakery - Union Square
notes: Flagship; super-flaky cinnamon sugar roll ($5.25).
review: "Brioche-based with a subtle sheen of vanilla and egg — an understated, refined roll from a bakery better known for babka."

name: Breads Bakery - Bryant Park
notes: Kiosk format; same roll at just $3.21, supply varies.
review: "Brioche-based with a subtle sheen of vanilla and egg — an understated, refined roll from a bakery better known for babka."

name: Chloe
notes: 100% vegan; reopened 2024 at original By Chloe space.
review: "A reliable vegan cinnamon roll that satisfies without dairy or eggs — pair it with one of their signature plant-based bowls."

name: Cloudy Donut Co. - Brooklyn Heights
notes: 100% vegan, Black-owned; Fri–Sun only.
review: "This vegan donut shop has no business making such a good cinnamon roll — tight swirl, great shape, way better than their donuts."

name: Cloudy Donut Co. - Nolita
notes: 100% vegan, Black-owned; Fri–Sun only.
review: "This vegan donut shop has no business making such a good cinnamon roll — tight swirl, great shape, way better than their donuts."

name: Benji's Buns
notes: Cinnamon-roll-only; open until midnight weekends, baked hourly.
review: "The original roll is extra doughy with strong cinnamon throughout — huge, you'll need a fork and a table."

name: Tall Poppy
notes: Australian-founded, small-batch; sells out by afternoon.
review: "All the elements of a Cinnabon but chewier and more textured — made from croissant dough scraps with thick glaze seeping into every layer."

name: Radio Bakery - Greenpoint
notes: The original from the Rolo's team; open until sold out.
review: "More pastry-with-coffee than gooey showstopper, but the lamination and baking quality are hard to beat."

name: Radio Bakery - Prospect Heights
notes: Newer location (Mar 2025); less competition for stock.
review: "More pastry-with-coffee than gooey showstopper, but the lamination and baking quality are hard to beat."

name: Welcome Home
notes: NYT Bakery of the Year; closed Tuesdays, sells out daily.
review: "Cinnabon's greatest impersonator — smothered with glaze, dark brown bottom, globs of sticky cinnamon sugar. Ask for a center piece."

name: Breadivore
notes: Wed–Sun only; babka-adjacent flaky layers.
review: "Filling so plentiful that every layer tastes like the center of a regular roll, with thick cream cheese frosting — great for sharing, if you must."

name: Petee's Pie Company - Clinton Hill
notes: Pie shop first; cinnamon roll is a pleasant surprise.
review: "Buttery, pie-crust-adjacent pastry with warm spice — open daily until 11pm, which is rare for a cinnamon roll fix."

name: Petee's Pie Company - Lower East Side
notes: Pie shop first; cinnamon roll is a pleasant surprise.
review: "Buttery, pie-crust-adjacent pastry with warm spice — open daily until 11pm, which is rare for a cinnamon roll fix."

name: Pies 'n' Thighs
notes: Southern comfort; cinnamon roll made from donut dough.
review: "Donut-dough cinnamon roll with cream cheese frosting — unapologetically indulgent, exactly what you'd expect here."

name: L'imprimerie
notes: French bakery; open daily 7am–6pm.
review: "Tastes like Christmas thanks to a heavy dose of cinnamon, with creamy cheesy icing and pleasantly flaky dough."

name: Cinnaholic
notes: Build-your-own vegan chain; evenings only (5pm+).
review: "Pick from dozens of frosting flavors and toppings on a fully vegan roll — not artisanal, but fun and customizable."

name: One Girl Cookies
notes: Cookie shop; new cinnamon roll at $4.75.
review: "Light, fluffy, and not too sweet — in a market of $7+ rolls, a $4.75 option with this much charm stands out."

name: Bub's Bakery
notes: Under-the-radar NoHo bakery; no lines.
review: "A solid everyday pastry stop without the hype or wait of trendier nearby spots — consistent quality across the board."

name: Yardsale Cafe
notes: Part café, part vintage shop; browse vinyl while you eat.
review: "Babka-adjacent pastry layers in a 4.9-star café where you can shop vintage finds — no other cinnamon roll spot has this vibe."

name: Noa, a Café
notes: Swedish-style cardamom bun; open daily 7am–4:30pm.
review: "Braided and spiced with cardamom — a completely different, lighter experience from the gooey American style, perfect with specialty coffee."
